items.title: feat: rewrite awake script from Jython to native Java
items.body:
## Summary
Converted the `awake` script from Jython (Python running on JVM) to native Java, eliminating the Jython dependency while maintaining full feature parity.

## Key Changes
- **Language migration**: Rewrote entire script from Jython to Java 11
- **Shebang update**: Changed from Jython execution to direct Java source execution using `--source 11` flag
- **Argument parsing**: Replaced argparse library with manual switch-case argument parsing
- **Time handling**: Converted from Python's `time` module to Java's `System.currentTimeMillis()` with proper millisecond-to-second conversions
- **Signal handling**: Replaced Python's signal handler with Java's `Runtime.addShutdownHook()` for graceful shutdown
- **Duration formatting**: Implemented custom `formatDuration()` method to replace Python's `timedelta` formatting
- **Class structure**: Organized code into a single `Awake` class with static methods
- **Build artifacts**: Removed `.jython_cache` from `.gitignore` as it's no longer needed

## Notable Implementation Details
- Maintains the same command-line interface with `-H`, `-M`, `-S`, and `-s` flags
- Preserves the 10-second check interval and 500ms sleep between iterations
- Uses `Point.equals()` for location comparison instead of Python's equality operator
- Properly handles time calculations in milliseconds while maintaining second-based duration logic
- Includes help text output matching the original argparse format

https://claude.ai/code/session_011cEH8XUGSF3Ue4gZe1sAbE

items.title: Update RundeckOptionModelResource.java
items.body:
ENGOPS-6596 adding tag, branches and filter

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes the REST contract for `getRefs` by adding new query parameters and conditional data retrieval, which can affect consumers and returned option lists. The new regex-based `filter` parameter also introduces potential runtime issues (and is currently applied before entries are populated).
> 
> **Overview**
> Extends the `options-provider` refs endpoint (`getRefs`) to accept new query params: `branches` and `tags` (defaulting to `true`) to control whether branch and/or tag refs are included, plus a `filter` regex to narrow results.
> 
> Ref retrieval is now conditional on these flags, with results still sorted and `selected` values marked as before.
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit a8b665f457002ae33552cc84bea01141d70274d3. Bugbot is set up for automated code reviews on this repo. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: feat(java-spring): add java-security skill
items.body:
Covers Spring Security 6 (Boot 3.x) and 5 (Boot 2.x):
- Review mode: audits existing config for CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM issues
- JWT mode: stateless JWT auth with JwtEncoder/Decoder (Boot 3.x) or manual OncePerRequestFilter (Boot 2.x)
- OAuth2 mode: resource server and login provider config
- Method security: @PreAuthorize patterns with examples
- CORS mode: global CorsConfigurationSource setup

references/patterns.md contains full code templates for all modes.

items.title: feat(java-spring): add java-openapi skill
items.body:
Covers OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for Spring Boot:
- Detect correct springdoc version (v2.x for Boot 3.x, v1.x for Boot 2.x)
- Generate mode: adds @Tag, @Operation, @ApiResponse, @Schema to all controllers and DTOs
- Review mode: audits existing docs for missing/wrong annotations
- Config mode: OpenApiConfig bean with JWT auth scheme, Swagger UI application.yml setup, production disable pattern

references/annotations.md contains full templates for controllers, DTOs, global config, and hidden endpoint patterns.

items.title: Update SensorHubOsgi.java
items.body:
Currently all bundles are being registered and started. We need to allow fragment bundles to be registered but this type of bundle does not require activation as it contributes functionality to a host bundle

This is an example of an osgi closure in a module that contributes custom forms to sensorhub-web-ui-core

```
osgi {
    manifest {
        attributes('Bundle-Vendor': 'Botts Innovative Research, Inc.')
        attributes('Bundle-SymbolicName': 'com.botts.ui.datafeed')
        attributes('Bundle-Version': version)

        // THIS is the important part:
        attributes('Fragment-Host': 'org.sensorhub.sensorhub-webui-core')

        attributes('-dsannotations': 'false')
        attributes('Export-Package': '')
        attributes('Import-Package': '*;resolution:=optional')
    }
}

```
In order to satisfy this SensorHubOSGi needs to skip attempting to start bundles that have the Fragment-Host attribute

items.title: Appmod/java upgrade 20260413215331
items.body:
## Summary by Sourcery

Integrate a Gemini-based AI adventure generation service, expose new AI-powered endpoints, harden JWT validation, and update build configuration for Java 21 with test-focused infrastructure improvements.

New Features:
- Add GeminiAdventureService for AI-powered adventure narration, mood prediction, chat assistance, and review sentiment analysis via the Gemini API.
- Expose new REST endpoints for AI-based adventure chat and mood prediction in AdventureController, using Gemini as the primary generator with fallback to the existing static service.
- Support generating multiple AI-generated adventures per request via the new service API.

Bug Fixes:
- Improve JWT token validation to safely handle malformed or invalid tokens without throwing exceptions.

Enhancements:
- Make AdventureController use Gemini-based adventure generation when available, falling back to the existing AdventureService for resilience.

Build:
- Upgrade project Java version from 17 to 21 in pom.xml.
- Add Gson and H2 test database dependencies to the Maven configuration.

Tests:
- Adjust AdventureController tests to mock UserRepository correctly and align authentication principal usage with production code.
- Mark AuthControllerTest and JwtUtilTest to use a dedicated test profile and reset Spring context between tests where needed.
- Introduce a test-specific application properties file configuring an in-memory H2 database, JWT secret, and email key for isolation and reliability.
